question_title: What rank will the top bot achieve in the Spring 2026 Metaculus Cup?
summary:
The top bot (most likely ManticAI) currently holds an equivalent rank of 5th in the Spring 2026 Metaculus Cup, with 4 human forecasters scoring higher. With ~19-23 questions remaining out of ~35 total scoring questions, there is meaningful variance in the final outcome.

Monte Carlo simulation across three model variants (independent scores, correlated scores with 12 humans, correlated scores with 14 humans) consistently produces a median of 5-6 and mean of ~5.85. The distribution is moderately right-skewed: reaching rank 1-2 is very unlikely (<2%) due to the 60-70 point gap to the top two humans, while falling to rank 8-10+ is plausible (~20%) if humans currently below ManticAI have strong remaining-question performance.

Key anchors: ManticAI's improving cross-tournament trend (8th→4th→currently 5th), the tight gap to Human4 (just a few points), and the substantial number of remaining questions creating uncertainty. The "top bot" definition (max of ManticAI, ChestnutForty, and any others) slightly favors a better rank than ManticAI alone.

factors.description: ManticAI currently 5th (4 humans above) with score 263.3, well-established position with 12/35 questions resolved
Historical improvement trend: 8th (Summer 2025) → 4th (Fall 2025) → currently 5th, suggesting strong competitive positioning
Significant 60-70 point gap to top 2 humans (334.1 and 323 vs 263.3) makes rank 1-2 very unlikely
~19-23 remaining questions provide substantial room for rank movement in both directions
Human4 barely above ManticAI (estimated ~268 vs 263.3), making rank 4 plausible; but humans below could also catch up
ChestnutForty (224.3) as second bot reduces downside risk - max of two bots slightly improves expected rank

reasoning:
**Forecast: Top Bot Rank in Spring 2026 Metaculus Cup**

**Median estimate: ~5-6th place** (80% CI: 3-9)

Key findings from my analysis:

- **Current position**: ManticAI sits at effective rank 5, with a score of 263.3 vs the leader at 334.1
- **Most likely range**: Ranks 4-7 capture ~67% of probability mass
- **Upside scenario** (rank 3-4, ~28%): ManticAI overtakes Human4 (who is barely ahead) and possibly Human3, aided by remaining question variance
- **Downside scenario** (rank 7-10, ~25%): Multiple humans currently below ManticAI catch up over the remaining ~19-23 questions
- **Extreme upside** (rank 1-2, ~2%): Very unlikely given the 60-70 point gap to the top 2
- **Extreme downside** (rank 11+, ~3%): Unlikely given ManticAI's established scoring advantage over most humans

The simulation was robust across model variants (independent vs correlated scoring, different human population sizes), consistently centering on rank 5-6.

resolution_criteria: This question will resolve as the constructive rank of the highest-scoring bot on the Spring 2026 Metaculus Cup leaderboard after all questions other than this one and [https://www.metaculus.com/questions/41508/cp-beats-nathan-young-in-the-spring-2026-cup/](https://www.metaculus.com/questions/41508/cp-beats-nathan-young-in-the-spring-2026-cup/) have been resolved.
fine_print:
Because bots are not ranked on the leaderboard, this question will resolve as 1 plus the rank of the lowest-ranked human forecaster with a score higher than that of the highest-scoring bot. (For example, at present, there are 4 human forecasters with scores higher than the score of the highest-scoring bot, and so the question would resolve as 5.) If no human forecasters outscore the highest-scoring bot, the question will resolve as 1.

***
This question's information (resolution criteria, fine print, background info, etc) is synced with an [original identical question](https://www.metaculus.com/questions/42913) which opened on 2026-03-27 17:00:00. This question will resolve based on the resolution criteria and fine print of the linked original question. However, if this question would resolve differently than the original question, then this question will be annulled. Additionally, if the original question's resolution could have been known before this question opened, then this question will be annulled.
